lifecycles._note: Light governance lifecycle for loggingaudit (audit trails / CloudTrail-equivalent). Covers all 6 uncovered writes: createtrail(POST /v1/trails), settrail(PUT /v1/trails/{trail_id}), starttrail(POST /v1/trails/{trail_id}/start), stoptrail(POST /v1/trails/{trail_id}/stop), deletetrail(DELETE /v1/trails/{trail_id}), downloadlogs(POST /v1/logs/download). No data/api_bodies.json entry for loggingaudit and docs are JS-rendered, so create/set bodies (target object-storage bucket + log selectors) are BEST-GUESS and UNPROVEN — promote after a real 2xx. createtrail typically requires a real object-storage bucket to receive logs, so a 400/403/422 is expected on a shared account; every mutating step is optional with a broad expect_status so it RECORDS coverage without failing. trail_id capture path $.trail.id is a guess; if absent the id-bound steps still normalize to the catalog '*' key. start/stop are POSTs without a body. downloadlogs is a coverage-only POST (it may stream a file on success; we only assert it is reachable). DELETE is destructive (gated).

steps.name: create-trail
steps._note: Body UNPROVEN — createtrail likely needs a pre-existing object-storage bucket. On the expected 4xx the optional flag isolates the group; the POST is still recorded as covered. tags[] receives owner tags via the engine.

steps.name: delete-trail
steps._note: If create captured no id, {trail_id} stays literal and the DELETE normalizes to the catalog '*' key (records coverage) and 404s harmlessly.

steps.name: download-logs
steps._note: Coverage-only POST. Body UNPROVEN (downloadlogs likely takes a trail/time-window selector). On success it may return a download URL or stream; we only assert reachability. Isolated group so a 4xx never affects the trail group.
